Manchester City’s Savinho and Cologne’s Said El Mala are two other names appreciated but likely to be out of Barcelona’s price range. Leverkusen’s Eliesse Ben Seghir has also been looked at in previous windows.

And on the right side, PSG’s Ibrahim Mbaye is another player tracked by Barcelona, but there has been no encouragement to date the French champions are willing to sell, and Barcelona are focused on the left side this summer.

Barcelona need a list of alternative targets because it is unlikely Manchester United change their position on Rashford, at least not so far out from the summer window opening, but Rashford is believed to be optimistic he can stay in Spain and feels he remains Barcelona’s top left-sided target.

United remain keen to get Rashford's wage permanently off their books, and it is not thought a new manager will change this position.

Rashford was informed by Manchester United last summer that he had no future at Old Trafford. He now has just over two years left on his current deal, and it would require a major U-turn from the club if they decide to reintegrate him into the first-team squad should Barcelona fail to make the signing permanent.

Football.title: Little-known Serie A starlet tipped to snatch Scotland’s No.9 shirt ahead of World Cup
Football.content:
Scotland could find a World Cup hero in Kieron Bowie after he proved a star man for Hellas Verona with his first goal in Italy.

The two-cap international, who made the move to Serie A in January, opened his account for his new club in a 2-1 win away to Bologna.

4 Bowie scored his first Serie A goal with Scotland team-mate Lewis Ferguson watching on Credit: Getty

The 23-year-old struck the winner for the relegation-threatened Gialloblù on Sunday to complete the club's surprise comeback win.



His first goal in Italy helped end Verona's 12-game winless run and lift the club three points clear of bottom side Pisa in the standings.

"I think it has been super difficult for him because Verona are not quite sunk but are pretty close to it," European football expert Andy Brassell told talkSPORT.com.

"The fact that he is off the market at last is great. He has come into a team that has been absolutely desperately low on confidence.

"But a team that I think are a little bit better than their results globally suggest."



Bowie - who was making only his fifth start since leaving Hibernian - has now scored 10 times in 35 club appearances in total this season.

The 23-year-old is still waiting for his first goal at senior international, having been handed his full debut last June.



However, Bowie has already hinted that he has an eye on gatecrashing Steve Clarke's squad for the World Cup this summer.

He said: “A striker’s job is to score, and scoring the winner is very satisfying, especially after a few chances I’d missed...



“As for Scotland, I hope to play the friendlies this month, do well there and then potentially play my cards right for the World Cup.”

4 Bowie hasn't been shy about voicing his hopes to make Scotland's World Cup squad Credit: Getty

Bowie for Scotland

Trans Europe Express regular Brassell has been a regular advocate for Oliver Burke to be the Tartan Army's World Cup wildcard.

Yet he admitted that Bowie's age, being five years younger than his compatriot at Union Berlin, puts the former at a massive advantage.

"I have seen a fair bit of Kieran Bowie at Hibs and I really liked him there as well," Brassell added to talkSPORT.com.



"Slightly different sort of system because he is edging towards playing in a two with Gift Orban. We will see how that develops.

"That could only help his game having that versatility. You look at Scotland, we have mentioned Oliver Burke earlier this season.

4 Brassell has previously made the case for Burke to earn a Scotland recall

"The fact that they could do with someone who is a bit of presence and a bit better than what they have already got.

"We know Scotland have got really good players in midfield and some really good players in defence as well. Up front, the centre forward spot is up for grabs.



"Even though Bowie was not relentlessly consistent with Hibs, there was enough to suggest that there is a player there.

"With the right coaching, with the right development, with the right team around him, he can become a really good player.

"He is already showing signs of that."

4 Steve Clarke has a decision to make over his misfiring attack Credit: Getty

Bowie vs Burke

Bowie is now into double figures across 35 appearances this term, but only his most recent strike came in one of Europe's top five leagues.

Burke, meanwhile, who won the last of his 13 caps in 2020, has scored four goals and registered one assist in 22 Bundesliga matches.



"If you are looking forward for Scotland, I have said a few times that I really like the idea of them taking Oliver Burke as an option.

"If you are looking to the medium term and long term future of Scotland, Bowie is just younger, isn't he? There is more to develop.

"If you are making a choice between those two, I suppose Bowie would be the clearer one. I am really interested to see what they do.

"If he finds some form then, he will be in the mix for Scotland."

News.title: Boy, 15, who ‘stabbed 15-year-old girl in back with large carving knife in school toilet area’ appears in court
News.content:
SCHOOL HORROR Boy, 15, who ‘stabbed 15-year-old girl in back with large carving knife in school toilet area’ appears in court

A TEEN boy has appeared in court charged with stabbing a 15-year-old girl in the back with a carving knife in a school toilet area.

The 15-year-old, who can’t be named, was arrested on Wednesday after armed police swooped on Thorpe St Andrew School in Norwich.





He appeared at Norwich Magistrates’ Court today charged with attempted murder and possession of a knife on school premises.

Prosecutor Josephine Jones said CCTV of a toilet area at the school showed a boy “all in black wearing a black mask” carrying a “carving knife with a lengthy blade”.

She added: “The victim had been stabbed to the back.”

Ms Jones said the defendant fled but was arrested by police nearby as the school was placed in lockdown.

A search at his home allegedly showed information that he was “looking for specific victims”, the court was told.

The teen, who spoke only to confirm his name, address and date of birth, was remanded into youth detention accommodation.

He will next appear at Norwich Crown Court on April 10.

The horror unfolded at around 10.20am on Wednesday after the girl was stabbed.



Teachers barricaded classroom doors and some students hid under desks as the school was plunged into lockdown.

The girl was taken to hospital with minor injuries and has since been discharged.

Norfolk Police have said they are continuing to conduct reassurance patrols both at the school and in the area over the coming days.

Thorpe St Andrew is an 11-18 school, plus sixth form, around three miles east of Norwich.

The mixed secondary school received a “Good” rating in its last Ofsted report.

News.title: M5 closed outside major UK airport as air ambulance lands after ‘11-vehicle’ crash with drivers warned of long delays
News.content:
THE M5 has been closed near a major UK airport as an air ambulance lands following a “11 vehicle” crash.

At least two people have been injured, according to the police.



Drivers have been warned to expect long delays.



It happened on the southbound carriageway between junction 28, for the A373 Honiton Road at Cullompton, and junction 29, for the A30 at Exeter, at around 11.30am.

Ambulance and fire crews are also at the scene, near to Exeter airport.

One witness has said the crash involved “multiple vehicles” and looked “very serious”.

Another witness said: “Crash between Cullompton and Exeter.”

Devon and Somerset Fire and Rescue Service said it had sent three appliances.

A spokesperson said: “One person was trapped and cut free of their vehicle by crews using hydraulic cutting equipment, they have been transported to hospital by air ambulance.”

National Highways said the southbound carriageway has been closed to allow emergency services to work safely at the scene.

It has not given an estimate for when the road will be reopened.



Motorists are urged to find alternative routes and to avoid the area.

It said on its website drivers should exit the M5 southbound at J28 and turn right at the traffic light controlled junction to join the A373 and follow diversion signs.

News.title: First known photograph of Andrew, Mandelson & Epstein together uncovered in US government files
News.content:
A NEW picture appears to show Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and Peter Mandelson sitting in bathrobes alongside Jeffrey Epstein.

It is believed to be the first known image of the three men together and was found in the Epstein files released earlier this year.





The three men can be seen sitting around a wooden table on some decking outside.

It is understood to have been taken between 1999 and 2000 before Epstein was imprisoned for soliciting prostitution from a minor.

The photo is believed to have been taken at Martha’s Vineyard, reports ITV.

The picture was included in the dossier of thousands of files on Jeffrey Epstein released by the US Department of Justice.

Mandelson and Andrew appear to be seen wearing white bathrobes while Epstein is dressed in a shirt and jumper.

The image bears similarities to another picture featuring Mandelson in a similar white robe sitting alongside Epstein.

The previous image, showing the disgraced Labour peer, was uncovered in a file labelled “Epstein birthday book.”

The extent of the relationship between Epstein and the two men was uncovered earlier this year when the US Department of Justice released thousands of documents relating to the paedophile financier.



Following the document dump Lord Mandelson was sacked from his role as US Ambassador.

Meanwhile, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor was stripped of his royal titles and removed from his stately country pad.

Both Peter Mandelson and the former prince have since been arrested over allegations of misconduct in a public office.

Both men have strongly and consistently denied any wrongdoing.

Andrew was arrested on suspicion of misconduct in a public office at around 8am on his 66th birthday.



The shamed ex-prince was quizzed in custody before he was released under investigation from the Aylsham police station at around 6.50pm later the same day.

The force previously said it was reviewing claims Andrew allegedly shared sensitive information with his paedo pal Jeffrey Epstein while serving as the UK’s trade envoy.

Cops also confirmed at the time that the force was separately probing allegations a woman was trafficked to the UK by Epstein to have a sexual encounter with Andrew.



After the bombshell arrest officers went on to raid other royal residences in Windsor and Norfolk.

Essex Police, the Metropolitan Police, Surrey, Thames Valley, Norfolk and Bedfordshire Police are all understood to have been assessing evidence within the files’ three million pages.

Their checks include up to 90 flights into Stansted’s private terminal by the jet owned by Andrew’s paedo pal.

Of those, 15 were after the US financier was jailed in 2008 for soliciting underage sex.

Cops earlier raided two properties linked to Lord Peter Mandelson — one in London and another in Wiltshire.



Mandelson was later arrested on suspicion of misconduct in a public office.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Documents in the Epstein files appeared to show the former ambassador passing confidential information to Epstein while he was Business Secretary.

News.title: Woman slapped with £150 fine for feeding tiny corner of McDonald’s wrap to hungry pigeon by council’s ‘litter police’
News.content:
COO KNEW! Woman slapped with £150 fine for feeding tiny corner of McDonald’s wrap to hungry pigeon by council’s ‘litter police’

A WOMAN has blasted “jobsworth” council after being hit with a £150 fine for feeding a pigeon a tiny scrap of her McDonald’s wrap.

Enforcement officers swooped in and accused the woman of littering after sharing a piece of tortilla with the hungry bird.





Sam Ekins from Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, was fined by two council officers when she fed a peckish pigeon in Manchester city centre.

The 23-year-old was visiting the city for the first time to celebrate her partner’s birthday, Rose, 24, when she sat in Piccadilly Gardens and ate her meal.

As she fed the bird, two Environmental Enforcement Agents pounced and told her she had broken the rules.

They are contracted privately by Manchester City Council and have been branded the “litter police” by locals.

Sam, who works as a Wetherspoons chef, said: “We got McDonald’s Wrap of the Day and sat a few feet from the entrance eating it.

“A pigeon came shooing up and I just thought I’d feed it. I didn’t think twice about pulling a tiny bit of my wrap off.

“The next thing I knew these two big security guards came out of nowhere saying they’re from Manchester City Council.

“They said I shouldn’t have discarded food and that I was liable for a £150 fine.



“I couldn’t believe it. It was literally just pulling off bits of a tortilla. It’s not like I chucked a whole meal on the floor and legged it.”

Sam said she immediately apologised and explained she was new to the city and unaware she had broken any rules.

She also claimed that one of the officers first seemed prepared to let her off – before suddenly changing tack.

“One of the men said something about letting us off with a warning but then asked me for my ID,” she said.

“Obviously when someone who looks like a law enforcement officer asks you for identification, the natural response is just to hand it over.



“Since he said he’d let us off with a warning, I assumed he understood that we didn’t know. But as soon as he had my ID, that was it.

“He started taking down the details and printed out a paper fine of £150.

“Because it was me and my partner, he said he’d let us off with just the one – as in he was going to fine us both. As if he was doing us a favour.

“I pointed out that the discarded food he was fining me for was long gone as the pigeon had eaten it and flown off.”

Sam has now launched an online fundraiser to help cover the fine, saying she will struggle to pay it by herself.



She said the couple are under financial pressure, with Rose dependent on disability benefits, while her own wages are just above minimum wage.

She added: “I do feel like they took advantage of two vulnerable people in a bad situation.

“I’m not some six-foot bloke like them – I’m 5ft 4in, and my partner uses a wheelchair.

“I think they are jobsworths. If they had any common sense whatsoever, they would have just warned me not to do it again.”

Manchester’s so-called litter police, previously from private contractor 3GS and now from the firm WISE (Waste Investigations Support & Enforcement), have sparked controversy before and been accused of being too heavy-handed.



A former charity worker was fined £150 for littering after giving a pigeon the crust of her ham and cheese sandwich in 2022.

Hazel Kirby, then 61, said the food vanished in seconds but was approached by a warden and ticketed.

In 2020, another woman who fed “tiny pieces” of her Greggs vegan sausage roll to pigeons in Piccadilly Gardens was also fined.

Enforcement officers handed Kerris Fenn a £150 fine – reduced to £120 if she paid it within ten days.

In the same year another woman was fined for “littering” after feeding pigeons a piece of tuna baguette she said was “the size of a £1 coin”.



A Manchester City Council spokesperson said: “The throwing of rubbish, including items of food, is classed as littering and where appropriate, enforcement officers will issue a Fixed Penalty Notice if an offence is observed.

“Signage has also been installed in some areas, warning against dropping food for the purposes of feeding pigeons or other birds.

“A Fixed Penalty Notice gives someone the option to deal with the alleged offence by paying the set fine, therefore avoiding prosecution through the courts and potentially, a much higher fine.

“Since receiving the Fixed Penalty Notice, Ms. Ekins has contacted the Customer Services team, and we can confirm that the matter has been placed on hold, so her case and circumstances can be fully considered.

“Littering of this type attracts vermin and can harm the amenity of Manchester’s public open spaces. Residents and visitors to the city have a shared responsibility in supporting our efforts to make the city cleaner and greener.”



But Sam insisted she never saw any warning signs and said she wants other visitors to know about the risk.

She added: “If you’re not from the area, you’re not going to know.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“The last thing you think if you drop a tiny bit of tortilla wrap is that you’ll end up with a £150 fine.

“We are going to appeal but sadly I think we will have to just bite the bullet and pay up.”

News.title: Domino’s superfan who got chain’s logo TATTOOED on his leg loses right to free pizza for life after campaign cancelled
News.content:
CRUST NOT FAIR Domino’s superfan who got chain’s logo TATTOOED on his leg loses right to free pizza for life after campaign cancelled

A PIZZA superfan who got the Domino’s logo tattooed on his leg has lost a court battle over a lifetime’s supply of free pizza after the chain’s promotional offer was cancelled.

Dmitry Mezentsev, who lives in Moscow, entered a competition called “Domino’s Forever” to win 100 free pizza vouchers for 100 years in exchange for a 2cm tattoo of the company’s logo.





Dmitry got a tattoo on his ankle, and was one of 350 winners eating free pizzas for the last five years – until Domino’s abruptly cancelled the scheme in 2023.

The company announced on Russian social media network VKontakte that the offer was no longer valid and that they would provide funding for tattoo removals.

Dmitry took the pizza mega firm to court – demanding the company continue to provide 100 28cm pizzas each year until 2118 and offer compensation for “moral damages”.



“I got sick of pizza”, confessed Dmitry, “but obligations to customers have to be fulfilled. It’s a matter of principle,” he told Moskva FM in 2023.

Moscow’s Meshchansky District Court did not agree, and ruled against Dmitry’s claim against Domino’s.

It’s not the first time the pizza giant has landed in hot water – or upset loyal tattoo-fanatics.

When the competition was first announced in 2018 on Facebook, Domino’s failed to set a time or participant limit, unleashing a wave of people inking their skin with the company logo.



Domino’s issued a panicked update, saying: “Friends, we already have 350 participants! We are not receiving any new tattoos!” as it scrambled to stop the onslaught of pizza tattoos by setting a one-week time limit.

Several customers expressed dismay and outrage after having uselessly tattooed their bodies with the signature blue-and-red before being told they were too late to receive a century’s worth of pizza.

The offer ended because Domino’s license was revoked following Putin’s invasion of Ukraine.

The chain’s 120 restaurants in Russia were taken over by restaurateur and rapper Anton Pinskiy, a man who once sang “my best friend is President Putin”.

News.title: Haunting pics show Dubai DESERTED with beaches empty & party pools silent amid Iran onslaught and free speech crackdown
News.content:
DISTURBING photos show Dubai deserted as Iran’s explosive onslaught continues and authorities clamp down on people sharing images of destruction on social media.

Once packed with influencers and tourists, many of the city’s best-known hotspots now stand silent as Iran continues to bombard the UAE.





Large numbers of residents and visitors have fled the city, with some vowing never to return as the Islamic Republic presses ahead with its campaign of retaliation.

Dubai’s iconic skyscrapers and luxury five-star hotels have been hit with debris from missiles and suicide drones.

Explosions rocked the city again on Friday as debris from intercepted drones tore chunks from buildings in the heart of the financial district.

At the centre of the desolation is Jumeirah Beach, within sight of the famous Palm archipelago.

Usually overflowing with holidaymakers, the stretch of sand now lies empty as many of Dubai’s 240,000 Britons make a desperate dash for the border.

It comes as…

Some beach clubs and restaurants have taken the bold decision to remain open, but their largely migrant workforce know that wealthy tourists from Europe and the US are nowhere to be seen.



One cafe manager told The Daily Mail: “I’ve never seen it like this before and everyone is worried what the future holds.

“The spring break for schoolchildren starts today and there is hardly a western child to be seen anywhere – their parents have all taken them home.”

Dubai has been the target of around 1,700 Iranian projectiles in the past two weeks, though roughly 90 per cent have been destroyed by air defences now working close to their limits.

In a desperate attempt to calm citizens, authorities told residents the “big booms” in the sky are simply the “sound of us being safe” as the air defence systems intercept incoming attacks.

Amid the chaos, thick black smoke could be seen billowing over an industrial area on Friday morning.



Officials later said the strike only caused minor damage and no casualties.

Earlier in the week, three huge explosions shook the city on Wednesday morning, with Dubai’s international airport among the sites hit.

Four people were injured when two drones crashed into the terminal, prompting several major airlines to cancel flights to the region.

However, the official Dubai Media Office insisted that “no incident” had occurred as authorities intensified their crackdown on people sharing images and videos of the attacks.

British nationals in the UAE have now been warned about the consequences of posting footage of the conflict.



The warning comes after a 60-year-old British tourist was charged under cyber-crime laws over alleged video showing missile strikes.

The man is accused of “broadcasting, publishing, republishing or circulating rumours or provocative propaganda that could disturb public security”, campaign group Detained in Dubai said.

The UAE's cryptic cyber-crime laws THE UAE’s cyber-crime laws are among the strictest in the world. People can face fines of up to £57,000, lengthy detention, and travel bans for social media posts that authorities consider harmful to “public order”, “national unity” or the reputation of the state. Detained in Dubai warns that the legislation is “deliberately broad” – and in times of war they become even more dangerous. Almost anything critical of government policy or regional conflict can be interpreted as a crime. In practice, commentary about regional conflict, government policy or security matters can be interpreted as a criminal offence. Sharing advice about what to do and what not to do in the country, the organisation said: “Do not assume your home country’s free speech protections apply in the UAE. They do not. “Do not assume that sharing international news coverage is safe. It may not be. “Do not assume that deleting a post removes liability. It often does not.”

On Friday the British Embassy posted on X: “UAE authorities warn against photographing, posting, or sharing images of incident sites or projectile damage as well as government buildings and diplomatic missions.

“British nationals are subject to UAE laws, violations may lead to fines, imprisonment, or deportation.”

Detained in Dubai said the tourist was one of 21 people charged under cyber-crime laws over videos and social media posts linked to the recent missile strikes.



The group said penalties in such cases can include up to two years in prison.

Iran’s attacks have also targeted US forces and their allies across the Gulf.

On Thursday, CENTCOM announced that a KC-135 refuelling plane had gone down in “friendly airspace” over Iraq.

Four of the six missing crew members have been confirmed dead while two remain unaccounted for.

Elsewhere, a major blaze appeared to erupt at a French air base in Erbil following a drone strike.



A helicopter was reportedly hit, injuring at least six French soldiers.

President Emmanuel Macron confirmed on Friday morning that one of them had died.

The escalating conflict has also triggered turmoil in global markets as the ongoing closure of the Strait of Hormuz fuels fears of a worldwide recession.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

The strategic shipping route normally carries around 20 per cent of the world’s oil supply each day.

Amid the mounting tensions, Ebrahim Zolfaqari, spokesperson for Iran’s military command, warned: “Get ready for oil to be $200 a barrel, because the oil price depends on regional security which you have destabilised.”

News.title: Princesses Beatrice and Eugenie still have homes in royal palaces as part of deal between Andrew & King Charles
News.content:
HOUSE OF YORK Princesses Beatrice and Eugenie still have homes in royal palaces as part of deal between Andrew & King Charles

PRINCESSES Beatrice and Eugenie will still have royal homes despite the scandal that’s engulfed their father.

A long-standing deal with King Charles means Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor’s daughters can keep renting their properties within royal palaces.





Andrew was forced out of his Royal Lodge home following his connection with convicted paedophile Jeffery Epstein, and has not had an office in Buckingham Palace since 2022.

Both Beatrice and Eugenie will be able to keep palace properties as their second homes thanks to a private arrangement between the Yorks and the King, reports The Times.

The deal reportedly requires the sisters to pay an undisclosed sum of rent, with the properties serving as a London base for them both.

Andrew’s eldest daughter, Beatrice, has an apartment in St James’ Palace.



However, she primarily lives at a £3million home in Oxfordshire with her husband Edoardo Mapelli Mozzi, a property developer, and their two daughters: Sienna, four and Athena, one.

The 37-year-old is also stepmother to her husband’s son, Wolfie, nine, from a previous relationship.

Eugenie has Ivy Cottage, a three-bedroom property at Kensington Palace, but spends most of her time at her home in Portugal.

The 35-year-old lives in the Mediterranean with her husband Jack Brooksbank, a marketing executive, and their two children: August, five, and Ernest, two.



Last year, it was revealed by The Mail on Sunday that Andrew previously rented a four-bedroom apartment in St James’ Palace for his daughters for a mere £1,600 a month – well below market value.

A property such as that in Kensington is estimated to cost between £4,000 and £15,000 a month, depending on the size and conditions of the rooms.

It is reportedly unclear if the princesses are now responsible for paying the rent of their royal properties.

These rental agreements are not available to the public, but the rent money allegedly goes back into the sovereign grant to fund the royal family’s official work.



The public accounts committee will investigate the rental agreements between members of the royal family and the Crown Estate later this year.

Despite being stripped of his title last year and arrested in February on his 66th birthday on suspicion of misconduct in public office, Andrew remains eighth in line to the throne.

This means all his grandchildren are higher in the order of succession than the Duke of Edinburgh or the Princess Royal – although none of them have royal titles.

Since being removed from Royal Lodge, Andrew has been residing on the Sandringham Estate at Wood Farm while a different, more permanent home is prepared.



This comes after an email leaked to the Mail on Sunday showed that Andrew pushed for his daughters to receive £100,000 in secret payments from a billionaire he was helping while trade envoy.

He discussed Beatrice and Eugenie getting £50,000 each in June 2011 with Jonathan Rowland, the son of his financier pal David Rowland, but it is unclear if the payments were ever made.

Meanwhile, Eugenie has had to step down as patron to Anti-Slavery International after emails released by the US Department of Justice laid bare her dad’s close ties to Jeffrey Epstein.

In a statement, the charity said: “After seven years, our patronage from HRH Princess Eugenie of York has come to an end.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“We thank the Princess very much for her support for Anti-Slavery International.



“We hope that she continues to work to end slavery for good and deliver freedom for everyone.”

News.title: Brits reveal top 30 moments they want to relive including first kiss & landing dream job
News.content:
ONCE IN A LIFETIME Brits reveal top 30 moments they want to relive including first kiss & landing dream job

Credit: Will Ireland/PinPep / SWNS

THE TOP 30 moments Brits would love to freeze and relive include their first kiss

The top 30 moments Brits would love to freeze and relive include their first kiss, landing their dream job and getting the keys to their first home.



Other milestone moments include holding their child for the first time, a magical Christmas morning from their childhood and hearing an ice cream van on a summer’s day.



A poll of 2,000 adults found 55 per cent admit to reminiscing about the past up to four times a week.

More than half (57 per cent) said they feel most nostalgic when looking back at moments like these, while 29 per cent even regularly revisit old photos or videos to help relive treasured memories.

The research was commissioned by Tesco Mobile, which today [March 12] encouraged the public to focus on meaningful moments by bringing a frozen van to King’s Cross to hand out complimentary frozen yogurts to passers-by.

Laura Joseph, chief customer officer at the phone provider, who hopes the frozen treats will also help lift the spirits of the public, said: “There are certain moments in time we would love to savour for a little longer.

“It lets us hold onto the feelings we felt at the time as well as preserving the emotion and magic of memories we never want to forget.”

The study, carried out via OnePoll, also found that when respondents were asked about freezing a current moment in time, 26 per cent would like to bottle up a special personal experience.

While for 43 per cent, stability and certainty in everyday costs is ‘very important’ to them.

Exactly half say it helps them manage their household finances and 48 per cent claim it reduces stress about unexpected expenses.



The spokesperson for Tesco Mobile, which is freezing Clubcard members’ basic monthly price for the duration of their minimum contract, added: “The research shows people aren’t just nostalgic for special moments, but for a time when everyday costs felt more predictable.

“With many wishing they could freeze prices as well as memories, it’s clear that stability plays a huge role in helping households feel more in control.

“So while we can’t help people relive their first kiss or childhood Christmas morning, we can help freeze their monthly bills.

“When costs are easier to manage, it can reduce stress and give people greater confidence in their day‑to‑day finances.”

News.title: 'I am a psychic and I’m urging people to buy a generator now'
News.content:
'I am a psychic and I’m urging people to buy a generator now'

Psychic Kelli, who claims to see into the future, has made alarming predictions about political upheaval in the US - and she warns people should buy generators now

View 3 Images The psychic's prediction could effect all homes in the US (Image: Daria Kulkova via Getty Images)

With US politics growing increasingly unpredictable and Trump's choices influencing major global developments, it's difficult to anticipate what lies ahead – so one psychic has made her predictions, and they're not entirely optimistic.

‌



In fact, her visions for what the remainder of 2026 might hold for the United States foresee considerable upheaval and "civil unrest."

‌



The psychic and medium has refined her abilities for over a decade and, utilising her unique talents to glimpse the future, has offered a forecast on political and social matters. One of which could directly impact every household across the US.

‌



Known as Psychic Kelli online, she had several predictions for the nation's future, one of which concerned electricity and energy supplies in homes.

@psychickelli on TikTok informed her followers: "I think there's going to be either a nationwide or even a statewide power outage. And I think they'll have a really lame excuse for it too."

View 3 Images A solar storm could be on the horizon (Image: BrianAJackson via Getty Images)

‌



She added: "So, if you've been thinking about getting a generator, it might be a good time."

Supporting her theory is Athos Salomé, a mystic whose precise predictions have earned him the title of the modern 'Nostradamus. ' He told the Express that in 2026 we can anticipate a massive solar storm, which could trigger a widespread power outage.

The unexpected eruption of particles, energy, magnetic fields and other matter being propelled into the solar system originates from the sun and carries significant consequences. He believes this could trigger a worldwide power blackout whilst also harming electrical infrastructure globally.

‌



The psychic told Salomé, "My direct and objective perspective is as follows: solar storms now seem like the most plausible scenario to have such an immediate impact on daily life, and backed by robust science.

"This deserves full focus. Not out of fear, but rather due to measurable signals in synchrony with potential power cuts, communication failures and technological disruptions in affected regions."

View 3 Images She suggests buying a generator (Image: Kinga Krzeminska via Getty Images)

‌



Kelli's forecasts were equally dramatic, with most pointing towards momentous developments, including those concerning the US President's wellbeing. She maintains she envisions Donald Trump having a fall, quite literally by tumbling and striking his head.

She went on to explain that the health incident will lead to hospitalisation, and we will witness JD Vance assume the role of president during his brief absence.

Extending her forecast even further, the psychic ventures to predict precisely when she anticipates this will occur - identifying August 12 as the date to note this year. In her view, this period coincides with when a solar eclipse falls within Leo, which can prompt unexpected revelations about those in authority.

Article continues below

She goes on to explain that this could be understood as revealing "those who deserve authority and those who can no longer be trusted." The psychic stated: "This is because pride, ego, and image are absolutely going to be tested."

News.title: Trump's war turning into a disaster spun into victory
News.content:
Trump's war turning into a disaster spun into victory

US President Donald Trump claims victory in the Iran war while Tehran continues hanging on and the US-Israeli attack is being met with mounting deaths and economic and political damage

View 6 Images Iranians scream anti-west slogans and burn Trump and Netanyahu imagery (Image: FAROOQ KHAN/EPA/Shutterstock)

Up to 3.2 million people have been temporarily displaced across Iran, driven out of their homes by devastating US-Israeli strikes, according to the UN today. Coalition military deaths are mounting as Tehran’s policy of spreading violence across the region digs in, indicating a protracted war could now be underway.

‌



And yet still Donald Trump claims the war is won and that US - Israeli forces are “destroying” the Iranian regime. Civilian deaths are soaring, with perhaps 1,300 killed so far and 17,000 injured and around the region, with at least 12 killed and many injured. Four more US troops died as their Stratotanker refuelling plane crashed in western Iraq and a French soldier also died in drone attacks on one of France’s Iraqi bases.

‌



View 6 Images US President Donald Trump's early victory claims sound extremely hollow (Image: Pool/ABACA/Shutterstock)

‌



As the casualties mount the war will become even more unpopular with America. Still more warplanes are arriving in UK bases, with British troops forced to take cover as drones smashed into their quarters in Erbil, northern Iraq.

The UK government is treading a desperately thin line, adopting a defensive posture, as Tehran’s regime lashes out, desperate to drag US allies into the conflict. Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps leaders do not acknowledge the blurred line between defensive and offensive action and want western voters opposing the war.

‌



View 6 Images B1 bombers from US at UK base RAF Fairford (Image: Getty Images)

View 6 Images American F-18 fighters in action (Image: US NAVY/AFP via Getty Images)

It is known that Russia has been feeding intelligence to the Iranians on how to be more effective, perhaps helping them with targeting. But it is also suspected that Russia has been assisting with drone tactics and missiles on how to make these projectiles more effective.

‌



Coalition troops have said already that the drones are flying unusually low , making them difficult to detect and this is something Russia maye have learned on the Ukraine frontlines. And this could surely mean a difficult choice for the British government who, thus far, have refused categorically to say the UK absolutely will not be joining in further in the war.

View 6 Images Type 45 Destroyer HMS Dragon leaves for the Med (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

US President Donald Trump and his government are hoping this war will end in a “few weeks” having already claimed victory. Claiming it does not make it true and despite his bullish outpourings Iran has a choice in when this war ends too.

‌



It seems Iran is soaking up some devastating attacks whilst letting the US and Israel spend billions on a war that they will not be able to sustain. All they can do is inflict as much damage as possible whilst the regime, for the time being, survives and hangs on. And an end to it seems remote at the moment.

View 6 Images US Navy and a Super Hornet jet (Image: Getty Images)

For the moment it is virtually impossible amid Trump's chaotic outbursts about the war to tell what the aim of it is actually , beyond imposing US military might on Iran, since regime change is not happening. Each leader will simply be replaced.

News.title: Jonathan Dimbleby warns Lords it is 'inhumane' to block assisted dying bill
News.content:
Jonathan Dimbleby warns Lords it is 'inhumane' to block assisted dying bill

Documentary legend Jonathan Dimbleby wants to see a change in the law after watching his brother die from motor neurone disease (MND).

View 2 Images Broadcaster Jonathan with his late brother Nicholas who died of MND and wanted him to campaign for the assisted dying bill (Image: LOFTUS MEDIA)

Broadcaster Jonathan Dimbleby described the Lords as "inhumane" for trying to block the Assisted Dying bill.

‌



The documentary maker is now urging the public to help stop this "travesty" and sign a petition forcing the “Government to do everything in its power…to ensure the unelected Lords cannot block them.”

‌



He points out there have been more than 1,000 amendments tabled to the Terminally Ill Adults (End of Life) Bill, most by just a handful of Lords. So far almost 90,000 have signed the petition. At 100,000 signatures it will be considered for debate in Parliament

‌



King Charles’ pal has described the current laws in the UK as “anachronistically cruel as capital punishment.”

The broadcaster’s younger brother, Nicholas, died with debilitating motor neurone disease (MND) in 2024 aged 77. He had been diagnosed almost exactly a year before but ended up unable to walk, eat and struggled to talk.

View 2 Images Jonathan Dimbleby has warned this is a "critical moment" (Image: Ken McKay/ITV/REX/Shutterstock)

‌



Mum died after routine op when hospital gave her the wrong medication READ MORE :

In a statement released through Dignity in Dying, the historian, 81, said: “This is a critical moment. Only four days remain for the House of Lords to return the assisted dying Bill to our elected MPs in the Commons. “By the end of this time, peers are supposed to have completed their examination of more than a thousand proposed amendments, determined which should proceed and which should be thrown out, and have had a final vote on the Bill. “Yet we are witnessing a travesty of this procedure. After an unprecedented ten days of debate, hundreds of amendments have yet to be discussed.

“At the current rate there is a grave risk that there will be no final vote before the end of the parliamentary session in May. In that case, the current Bill would be brought to a juddering halt.”

He urged the public to sign the petition to “ensure that the unelected Upper House does not thwart the will of the people”.

‌



“My brother, Nicholas, recently died from motor neurone disease. There is no cure and, as yet, no treatment for MND which is invariably fatal. In the final months of his life, with his body rapidly wasting away,

“Nicholas was unable to speak or to swallow. But he had a clear mind. While Nicholas would never dream of speaking for anyone else with this disease, the loss of control was humiliating, the indignity was hard to bear. It took rare courage.

“A courage which should not be forced on anyone. I do not know whether he would have chosen an assisted death but I do know he believed passionately in the right to choose and wanted me to argue for it. “Throughout this campaign, I have been greatly moved by the quiet determination of those who are terminally ill and of those who love them.

‌



“They have spoken in public with extraordinary dignity about their suffering. They have asked only that Parliament listen and act. “I respect the views of those who oppose the Bill and would never exercise the right to choose an assisted death for themselves. But I have no respect for their attempt to deny that right to the rest of us. That is not only undemocratic, it is inhumane. “

He added: “The progress of the Bill so far has been blocked by a handful of Peers whose opposition to it is absolute and unyielding.

“They have not only tabled many hundreds of amendments but they are using these to prolong the proceedings and thereby to prevent their colleagues from having a final vote. “This is filibustering: a series of wrecking amendments designed to frustrate the expressed will of our elected MPs in the Commons. I believe this is wrong in principle but I also know what it would mean in practice. “

Article continues below

“To ensure that the assisted dying Bill become the law of the land, we must not give up the campaign.”

News.title: 'Serial rape liar Stacey Sharples ruined our lives'
News.content:
'Serial rape liar Stacey Sharples ruined our lives'

Stacy Sharples made up claims against men she met on dating apps. She was jailed for four-and-a-half years after pleading guilty to 10 counts of perverting the course of justice

View 2 Images Stacey Sharples falsely accused 10 men of rape

Men falsely accused of rape by a woman’s “wicked pack of lies” have told how she ‘ruined’ their lives.

‌



False accusations made by Stacey Sharples, 31, cost people their jobs, wrecked relationships, saw men disowned by family and friends and drove a wedge between some of them and their children. Many of the men spent hours in police custody and months on bail. None were ever charged.

‌



Sharples was jailed for four-and-a-half years this week after pleading guilty to 10 counts of perverting the course of justice. She lied about being attacked by 10 men from across Greater Manchester between 2013 and 2019.

‌



View 2 Images Sharples caused agony with her 'wicked pack of lies' (Image: Facebook)

Killer buried his wife under flowers bought from B&Q after savage knife murder READ MORE :

Nine of those men gave emotional victim impact statements at Bolton Crown Court - detailing how wrongly being branded a rapist had impacted their lives.

The Honorary Recorder for Bolton Judge Nicholas Clarke KC told her: “They were untruly and unfairly branded as rapists within their families, their places of work, in their areas and on social media.”

‌



In the first of the victim statements Reece Lockett said his parents completely cut all ties with him after Sharples made up the rape allegations: “My mum and dad disowned me. My partner of five years left me. I lost my part-time job.”

He added: “People messaged me on Facebook accusing me of something I didn’t do, and that made me feel isolated and ashamed, even though I knew the truth.”

Another victim, Kaylum Davis, said: “Some of my friends fell out with me over this accusation. I still think about this to this day.Now it's all out in the open and her lies have caught up with her, it’s made me speak about it more as it’s easier to explain to people due to all the other individuals in this case have said their part, who were also wrongly accused."

‌



Andrew Dearden said Sharples’ lies affected his mental health: “Although I was released with no further action for the allegations, sometimes I start to think about them, and my depression starts to get worse” adding: the ordeal has had “a detrimental effect on my life and caused me to not look after myself.”

A fourth victim, Astron Inman, said: “Words can’t describe the torment my head has gone through. I moved to Sweden not long after to re-evaluate my life and wasn’t a part of my children’s lives for a couple of years. I have been single ever since and I find it hard now to approach the opposite sex.”

Andrew Jackson said he was prevented from seeing his daughter throughout the course of the investigation: “My relationship with my daughter is really important to me and the fact I wasn't able to see her has had a significant impact on me.

‌



“I have missed out on key milestones and everyday moments, not being able to fix the situation quickly has caused me intense frustration and to feel powerlessness. I have worried about court outcomes for so long for something I know that did not happen.”

He added: “Being a parent is central to my identity and separation from my daughter felt like losing a part of myself.”

Jack Byrne said he was unable to “function normally” after Sharples made false allegations about him. He said: “I was signed off work due to stress and anxiety. I struggled with constant worry about my future, my reputation, and the possibility of losing everything I had built.

‌



“The stress affected my sleep, my appetite, and my overall mental health. I felt depressed, isolated, and ashamed - even though I knew I was innocent.”

Another man, Connor Austen, said Sharples' lies affected every aspect of his life. “When this came out I had no opportunity to explain to my partner at the time about the false accusations,” he said.

“I lost my home, my partner and had to defend myself for something I never did. I lost my job and couldn't get another one. It affects me still now in future relationships thinking, ‘can I trust them or am I gonna be accused again’. She ruined seven years of my life.”

‌



Another man who was falsely accused by Sharples, who did not want to be identified, said: “The arrest was difficult to process and as my family were present at the time this made it very difficult to explain the situation. This really strained relationships with family and friends and to this day still has an impact on my relationship with them.

“At the time my ex-partner also prevented me from seeing my child for the duration of the investigation. "As you can imagine these were extremely difficult times.”

A man, who was the last Sharples falsely accused, said: “Immediately prior to my arrest, I would say my life was the best it had ever been. I had been with my partner for ten years. I had a good job, and I had a nice house.

‌



“Soon after, due to circumstances out of my control, I became homeless. I began misusing alcohol – I was not drinking every day, but when I did have a drink, I would drink until I was black-out drunk to drown things out- the things going on in my own head.

“I would stay out until five in the morning – acting like an idiot and getting into fights. If I'm honest with myself, I'm still struggling with that now. Before my arrest, none of this was a problem.”

In court Judge Clarke said he hoped the case would not put genuine victims off from reporting crimes against them - a message backed by Greater Manchester Police.

‌



A police spokesman said: “False accounts such as these will impact the confidence genuine victims and survivors of rape have in the police and criminal justice system. Our message to them is when you are ready, we are here to listen to you and support you, not to accuse you of lying.”

They added; “False accounts also undermine those who have genuinely experienced sexual violence and confidence in the criminal justice system. The time spent investigating Sharples' reports could have been put towards investigating genuine reports of sexual offences.

“The amount of rape victims and survivors we have secured positive outcomes for has doubled since 2021, as we continue to be victim-centred and suspect-focussed, improving both the quality of our investigations and the support in which those who come forward receive.”

News.title: All the airlines hiking prices as jet fuel costs soar amid war in Middle East
News.content:
All the airlines hiking prices as jet fuel costs soar amid war in Middle East

Several airlines have announced that they already have or will soon raise the prices of some tickets as the cost of jet fuel soars

View 7 Images Some passengers will face more expensive ticket prices (Image: NurPhoto via Getty Images)

The ongoing conflict in the Middle East has sent the cost of jet fuel soaring while some travellers have been warned they could face rising fares.

‌



Several airlines have already confirmed that their prices will rise as the cost of aviation fuel goes up significantly just before summer. The huge Hong Kong-based airline Cathay Pacific has become the latest company to implement an emergency fuel surcharge to help offset the rapidly increasing costs.

‌



Iran has effectively blocked the Strait of Hormuz - where one fifth of the world's oil supply passes - by bombing some ships in the passage, as part of retaliatory strikes.

‌



View 7 Images Several airlines have revealed rising jet fuel costs will be partially passed onto flyers (Image: Getty Images)

British dad leaves UK to join Kurdish forces fighting the Iranian regime READ MORE :

Iran war ramps up - with British holiday hotspot now in the line of fire READ MORE :

A series of attacks on vessels in the waters close to the passage have been reported, with shocking videos showing cargo ships and tankers burning. The sudden chaos has sent the price of a barrel of oil to over $100, which has led to higher petrol prices and jet fuel prices.

The cost of a tonne of jet flue was around $830 before the conflict erupted. This price has unbelievably now soared to more than $1,500 a tonne. Air travel has also faced other disruptions with passengers stranded while airspaces across parts of the Middle East were closed for days, forcing flight to remain grounded.

‌



Some airlines have announced measures such as rising prices or new fees amid this travel nightmare:

United Airlines

The US airline's CEO, Scott Kirby, last week revealed the carrier's profits would take a "meaningful" hit due to trouble in the Middle East. He also warned that fares could soon rise if the price of jet fuel remains high.

View 7 Images The United Airline CEO said ticket prices could soon rise (Image: NurPhoto via Getty Images)

‌



In an interview with CNBC, he said: "You know I think in the longer term, it's always been the case at an airline that jet fuel prices go up, usually because demand is strong - and that's going to be true again. Higher jet fuel prices lead to higher ticket prices. Ultimately, we'll pass that through."

Qantas

The Australian airline revealed it would be raising the price of its international flights. In a statement, the airline said: "Qantas International is increasing fares this week in response to rising costs including the significant increases in jet fuel prices. These increases will vary from route to route."

Despite this, the airline also reported higher-than-usual ticket sales for long-haul flights to Europe.

‌



Cathay Pacific

The Hong Kong-based carrier said it added a £54 surcharge onto flights from Europe and North America in recent days. The airline said it will review these surcharges again next month.

CEO Ronald Lam, on Wednesday, revealed the price of fuel this month has been double the average, when compared to the previous two months. The company said this new fee will apply to tickets from March 18.

View 7 Images Cathay Pacific has already added a surcharge onto some fares (Image: Anadolu via Getty Images)

‌



KLM and Air France

The two partner airlines have increased their airfare prices. In a statement, the company KKM said increasing fuel costs "will impact ticket prices, with the increase varying by destination and class".

A spokesperson for the French carrier added that economy tickets would go up by £43 for a return journey.

Norwegian

A newspaper in Norway reported that the airline will adjust prices to include the rising cost of jet fuel.

‌



Air New Zealand

The New Zealand-based carrier was one of the first to announce prince increases - which includes an extra £8.80 for one way short-haul flights. There will be an extra £39 added to one way long-haul journeys.

View 7 Images The airline said it will cancel some flights (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

The airline also announced, on Thursday, that it would cut 5% of its flights - around 1,100 services - until the start of May, due to jet fuel concerns.

‌



Air Canada

A spokesperson for the airline, on Wednesday, said the cost of flights "has been and continues to be adjusted to reflect these higher fuel costs".

View 7 Images Air Canada said it has already 'adjusted' prices (Image: NurPhoto via Getty Images)

Air India

The airline, on Tuesday, said it was going to start a phased expansion of jet fuel surcharges both for domestic and international journeys.

‌



Norse Atlantic Airways

The budget airline - which has many routes from the UK to parts of the US, Thailand and South Africa - said the more expensive jet fuel prices have already been reflected in tickets.

Thai Airways

The airline said customers who haven't already booked their flights could expect a fuel surcharge cost of around 15% per ticket.

Hong Kong Airlines

The company revealed surcharges of up to a staggering 35% will be slapped onto ticket prices from Thursday.

‌



Air Asia

The budget Malaysian airline announced it would be bringing in a temporary surcharge amid the jet fuel cost crisis.

View 7 Images The Canadian airline will adjust ticket prices to Europe (Image: Toronto Star via Getty Images)

Air Transat

The budget Canadian airline said it will be adjusting prices for journeys to Europe. CEO Annick Guérard, in a call to investors on Wednesday, said: "We have increased fuel surcharges on Europe. However, this is blended in the total price. What we're also doing is currently raising fares on peak travel dates and routes where we see less competition, where we have more flexibility."

Article continues below

SAS Scandinavian Airways

The airline, earlier this week, said it has already changed flight prices due to rising fuel costs.

News.title: UKHSA issues Legionnaires' disease warning for people living in England
News.content:
UKHSA issues Legionnaires' disease warning for people living in England

The government agency shared a warning about the health risks associated with the 'serious' lung infection

View 2 Images People with the lung infection usually need hospital treatment (stock photo) (Image: Getty)

The UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) has warned Legionnaires' disease could be 'very serious' for some people in the UK, with three groups facing increased risk. Issuing advice, the government agency shared a social media post raising awareness of the lung infection, which is a severe form of pneumonia.

‌



According to the NHS, people get Legionnaires' disease by breathing in water droplets containing Legionella bacteria. While Legionnaires' disease is uncommon, it can be 'very serious'. Posting on X, UKHSA explained that the infection can be particularly serious for older adults, smokers, or people with underlying health conditions.

‌



In an awareness post, the agency said: "Legionnaires' disease is a serious type of pneumonia caused by the bacterium Legionella pneumophila. People contract the disease by breathing in fine water droplets containing Legionella." The post then outlined the three groups at heightened risk.

‌



The warning comes after UKHSA said it had recorded a rise in cases of Legionnaires' disease in parts of north-west and south-west London. An urgent investigation is underway.

Dr Janice Lo, consultant in health protection for London at the UKHSA, said: "At this stage, a common source has not yet been confirmed, but we are undertaking further investigations, with partners, that will provide us with more information.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



"Legionella bacteria is widely distributed in the environment and can multiply in large water systems within buildings. It is important that water systems are properly maintained with regular testing and treatment to ensure that bacteria do not multiply and cause a risk to health."

The NHS says Legionnaires' disease is usually caught in places like offices, hospitals, or hotels when bacteria get into the water supply. You can get Legionnaires' disease from air conditioning systems, spa pools and hot tubs, taps or showers that are not used often, and humidifiers.

You usually cannot get Legionnaires' disease from drinking water, other people with the infection, or places like rivers and ponds. As per the health service, symptoms of Legionnaires' disease include:

‌



a cough

shortness of breath

chest pain or discomfort, particularly when breathing or coughing

a high temperature

flu-like symptoms

View 2 Images Ask for an urgent GP appointment if you have symptoms (stock photo) (Image: Getty)

Legionnaires' disease is usually treated in hospitals, and most people make a full recovery. You should ask for an urgent GP appointment or contact NHS 111 if you experience symptoms of Legionnaires' disease, such as:

Article continues below

a cough that's lasted three weeks or more

coughing up blood

chest pain that comes and goes, or happens when breathing or coughing

feeling short of breath

It's important to tell the medical team where you have spent the last ten days, for instance, if you stayed in a hospital, spa or hotel. The UKHSA's primary responsibility is to protect the public's health in England.

News.title: Girl, 15, 'stabbed in back with carving knife' in school as pupils dial 999
News.content:
Girl, 15, 'stabbed in back with carving knife' in school as pupils dial 999

A 15-year-old boy has appeared in court charged with the attempted murder of a teenage girl who was stabbed in the back at a secondary school near Norwich.

The defendant, who wore a dark-coloured jumper and tracksuit bottoms, appeared in the secure dock at Norwich Magistrates' Court on Friday, together with three security officers.

He spoke only to confirm his name, address and date of birth, and was not asked to enter pleas to the charges of attempted murder and possession of a knife on school premises.

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

Prosecutor Josephine Jones said the defendant and the girl he is accused of attacking are both 15-year-old pupils at Thorpe St Andrew School.

Image: Newsquest / SWNS) Newsquest / SWNS)

She said a number of 999 calls were made on Wednesday and the school went into lockdown, with armed police sent to the scene.

The prosecutor said "CCTV of the toilet area shows a male all in black wearing a black mask" carrying a "carving knife with a lengthy blade".

She added: "The victim had been stabbed to the back."

Ms Jones said the defendant fled but was arrested by police nearby.

District Judge Matthew Bone put in place reporting restrictions meaning the identities of the defendant and the girl cannot be published.

He remanded the defendant into youth detention accommodation, with his next hearing to be at Norwich Crown Court on April 10.

The boy's father sat on a chair outside the secure dock, close to his son, as he listened to proceedings. The father wiped tears from his eyes at the end of the hearing, having been offered a box of tissues.

Police were called to Thorpe St Andrew School on Laundry Lane at 10.24am on Wednesday following reports that a teenage girl had been stabbed.

The girl was taken to hospital with minor injuries and has since been discharged.

News.title: Donald Trump health fears grow as he suffers 'memory lapse' on live TV
News.content:
Donald Trump health fears grow as he suffers 'memory lapse' on live TV

The US president has sparked fresh health fears after viewers spotted an unfortunate incident live on TV during one of his speeches. Earlier this month, people spotted another issue

View 8 Images Donald Trump appeared to suffer a blunder at the event (Image: Pool/ABACA/Shutterstock)

TV viewers have expressed concern for the US president after a worrying moment was caught live on air.

‌



Donald Trump was live at the White House to host an event for Women's History Month alongside his wife and First Lady Melania when he shouted out his former counsellor Kellyanne Conway. But in a wince-worthy clip, he appeared to suffer a a lapse of memory by mixing up Conway - who served as Senior Counsellor to the President from 2017 to 2020 - with his current press secretary Karoline Leavitt.

‌



In his gushing speech, POTUS thanked Conway, asking his audience: "And of course Kellyanne Conway, has anyone ever heard of her?

‌



He continued: "She's fantastic. She's in there fighting. A friend of mine said, 'You know that Kellyanne, I admire the way she goes in there and she screams at those people' – meaning the media, because this is a man who doesn't do very well with the media, it's just one of those things. But thank you, Kellyanne."

View 8 Images He seemed to confuse Kellyanne Conway with Karoline Leavitt (Image: Getty)

Viewers quickly expressed concern over Trump's apparent confusion, taking to social media to share their reactions. One user wrote: "He's confusing Karoline Leavitt with Kellyanne Conway? Am I hearing that correctly?

‌



Another said: "What is he talking about? Does he mean Leavitt? Omg. He's cooked". Meanwhile, a third questioned: "Why, why, why is the press not treating his obvious cognitive decline with even half the scrutiny they lavished on Biden?"

"Another simply stated: "Trump is not well," reports the Express. "He thinks everyone works for him," another viewer joked.

The worrying TV blunder is just the latest in POTUS' suspected health woes. Earlier this month, the president was spotted with nasty scabs on his neck during a Medal of Honour ceremony at the White House. The dark discolouration stretches across a large section of skin.

‌



The White House has repeatedly brushed aside concerns about the president's wellbeing after concerning behaviour from Trump during speeches and mysterious lumps, bruises and skin flare-ups. Here we take a look at all the fears in full...

Nasty scabs

Running more than 40 minutes behind schedule at the recent Medal of Honour ceremony, Trump once again displayed discoloured hands whilst presenting awards to military service members - as well as a new rash on his neck.

View 8 Images Sore, red rashes were spotted on Donald Trump's neck

‌



White House physician Sean Barbabella said: "President Trump is using a very common cream on the right side of his neck, which is a preventative skin treatment, prescribed by the White House Doctor. The President is using this treatment for one week, and the redness is expected to last for a few weeks."

The 79-year-old also appeared to stumble and slur his words whilst attempting to honour the final recipient of the ceremony. "Finally, we honour one more soldier, a fallen warria of world... of wars," he could be heard saying as he read from a teleprompter, the Mirror US reports.

'Death' rumours

The rumour mill went into overdrive in September, with many political watchers left convinced that President Donald Trump had died.

‌



The phrase "Trump is dead" went viral, with many left baffled that the US President hadn't been seen publicly for a few days, and appeared to have a clear diary for the Labour Day Weekend.

This followed a wave of speculation over the politician's health, despite his doctor Sean Barbabella having declared back in April that "President Trump remains in excellent health, exhibiting robust cardiac, pulmonary, neurological, and general physical function".

View 8 Images Donald Trump spotted with unusual lump under his suit

‌



Addressing the media yesterday on 2 September, Trump ridiculed rumours that he was no longer living, scoffing: "I have heard it's sort of crazy, but last week I did numerous news conferences, all successful. They went very well, like this is going very well, and then I didn't do any for two days, and they said there must be something wrong with him."

Taking the opportunity to have a dig at his old presidential rival, Joe Biden, Trump insisted: "Biden wouldn't do them for months. You wouldn't see him, and nobody said anything."

However, those watching on couldn't help but be distracted by another apparent 'clue' as to the president's wellbeing...

‌



Mystery lump

An unusual crease in Trump's suit sleeve, caught the attention of many reports the Mirror US, giving the appearance of some sort of bulge. One individual on X posted, "Strange. Fabric doesn't normally fall like that across an arm."

This message was accompanied by a photograph of Trump from his recent White House meeting, with the suspicious user highlighting what appeared to be a mysterious protrusion beneath POTUS's suit jacket. Other X users reasoned that the bulge resulted from a padded suit, however the original poster disputed this, going as far as to provide examples of other padded suits to demonstrate the difference.

View 8 Images The unusual lump has added fuel to the fire of speculations about his ill-health

‌



They continued: "People are suggesting this is a result of padded or extended shoulders. Neither of these things have to do with how a sleeve falls." Another person queried: "Why does such a wealthy man not have well-tailored suits?" A third individual also made the suggestion that the bulge resulted from someone supporting the president to keep him steady, mere moments before the photograph was taken.

Skin discolouration

Yesterday evening (September 2), Trump addressed members of the American public in a major announcement regarding Space Command HQ, but many were instead left discussing what appearaed to be discolouration on his hands. Sharing a screengrab from the annoucement, one sharp-eyed X user wrote: "Trump's hands are visibly 2 different colors today." Others were quick to comment on Turmp's voice sounding "weak", while one social media critic remarked that the Oval Office podium was "acting as a walker to help him stand up".

View 8 Images Donald Trump has had skin discolouration at numerous events (Image: Getty Images)

‌



In a statement, Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt declared that the President is "perfectly fine and has a tremendous amount of energy". She told CNN: “The made-up speculation online is crazy and baseless, and it’s clearly being pushed by Democrat activists and left-wing lunatics."

Bruised hands

On Monday, August 25, a dark bruise was spotted on Trump's hand as he signed an executive order in the Oval Office, mere days after golf video showed him to have a bruised hand. Another image at a seperate event appeared to show that POTUS had applied make up to the back of his hand.

Speaking to Reach PLC, Medical Director of Manual Dr Jeff Foster expressed concern over the recurrence of the bruises over such a lengthy period of time. According to Dr Jeff: “It appears this bruise in question is recurring, having seen a similar bruise in August and November of last year.

‌



View 8 Images A health expert notes that the bruises seem to be recurring (Image: Getty)

“The most likely explanation is that this could well be bruising from a recent cannula from the way it appears, and of course, as Donald Trump is nearly 80 years old, his skin is naturally thinner and weaker, which happens as we age, and therefore we bruise much more easily.

“The White House representatives have suggested Donald’s bruise is from constantly shaking hands with people; however, I would be concerned if this was indeed the case, as it could indicate the use of blood-thinning drugs or skin fragility.”

‌



Back in April, when the bruises first came to attention the University of Salford’s Dr Gareth Nye suggested that President Trump could be undergoing IV therapy.

View 8 Images Trump could be having IV therapy according to one expert (Image: Getty)

Dr Nye advised the Daily Star: "Bruising is much more common as you age due to the thinning of skin and weakening of blood vessels. As we age, even minor bumps can result in bruising, and there's often very little underlying to suggest a health concern.

Article continues below

"This is more likely if a person is on blood-thinning medication or has been exposed to excessive sunlight. It could also be a completely benign condition called actinic purpura, which is also caused by excessive sun exposure. It does resemble the type of bruising left by IV drip insertion, although there's no evidence of IV usage or need."

Back in July, the White House confirmed President Trump was suffering from a chronic venous insufficiency, with Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt revealing that the 79-year-old had undergone a "comprehensive exam" including vascular testing after suffering swelling in his legs.

Leavitt went on to add that Trump's bruised hand was in fact consistent with "tissue damage from frequent handshaking" while taking aspirin, which she asserted is "part of a standard cardiovascular prevention regimen".

'Iran's women are heroes - they want to be free'

The Iran women's national football team have become "heroes" and deserve to be free to live their lives, says former Iran men's head coach Afshin Ghotbi.

News.title: Mission accomplished? The 2003 boast that haunts today's Iran conflict
News.content: Iraq is now in a much better state than it was in the immediate aftermath and many are glad to see Saddam Hussein gone. But democracy did not spread through the Middle East in the aftermath as some had claimed it would. Instead, one of the biggest winners of the invasion would be Iran whose main adversary was removed, allowing it to extend its influence into Iraq and beyond in the years after the war. And it would increase the terrorist threat within the UK and the broader West. Wars do not always have the outcomes people expect or want.

However, every car sold in the UK, regardless of size, is certified to exacting safety and pedestrian protection standards - part of the reason why the UK has one of the world's best road safety records.

Boxing.title: Jake Paul suffers embarrassing hygiene gaffe during Donald Trump rally appearance as major wardrobe error exposes long-held insecurity
Boxing.content:
YouTuber-turned-boxer Jake Paul was seen profusely sweating in an appearance with US President Donald Trump at a political rally.

Paul joined Trump on stage at Verst Logistics in Kentucky and was given the chance to address the crowd.

The 29-year-old went viral on social media, not for his comments about Trump and American manufacturing, but instead about his appearance.

Many pointed out vast sweat patches that were visible on Paul's beige suit as he spoke to the crowd.

The patches were particularly prominent under his armpits, which were exposed further when he raised his arms to the crowd.

Paul had previously spoken about how sweating had long been a major insecurity of his.

Jake Paul gets on stage at the Trump rally and has the nastiest pit stains I've ever seen



(it also appears the crowd doesn't know who he is) pic.twitter.com/3Yc071tZzf — Aaron Rupar (@atrupar) March 11, 2026

YouTuber-turned-boxer Jake Paul was seen profusely sweating in an appearance with US President Donald Trump

Paul and his brother Logan had promoted his deodorant brand ahead of his fight with Mike Tyson back in 2024

Paul made the revelation back in 2024 when he launched a deodorant brand W, which he and his brother Logan promoted before and after his bout with Mike Tyson.

'Sweating has been a longtime insecurity of mine,' Paul had said when launching the brand.

'If you know me, you know I'm a clean freak. I always want to smell fresh, but training in Puerto Rico's humid climate makes it a real challenge.

'I needed a product that could keep up with my lifestyle — one that smells amazing but isn't full of junk ingredients, and honestly, one that wasn't from 2006.

'That's why I started W… to create products for guys that will boost their confidence and smell amazing, but also don't cost $20 for a stick of deodorant.'

The W deodorants are claimed to offer 24 hours protection, with the brand website stating: 'With doubled scent load, increased hydration and more sweat-blocking power, our new deodorant and body wash are a monumental breakthrough for mankind (or at least for your morning)'.

During the event, Trump signalled that Paul's influence could eventually shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government in the coming years.

'I'm going to make a prediction that you will be, in the not too distant future, running for political office,' Trump told the fighter and the assembled supporters.

Join the debate Did YOU spot Jake Paul's hygiene gaffe?

Trump had brought Paul onstage during a rally on Wednesday in Kentucky

Trump suggested that Paul could shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government

'And you have my complete and total endorsement.'

Paul had expressed his admiration for Trump credited him with teaching him the fundamental values of grit and resilience.

'We need more factories like this thriving all over the country,' Paul added. And I know [Trump's] going to be the one to bring that here to us.

'I know God is with us. I know he wants us on the right side of history, and everyone here has to do their part.

'And God's got us. Trump's got us. God bless.'

Trump returned the praise, telling the crowd: 'He's a great guy. He's a courageous guy and a talented guy. He's a hell of a fighter, too, by the way.'



The endorsement of Paul's fighting spirit comes as the 29-year-old continues to dominate headlines following his endeavours in the boxing ring over recent years.

Boxing.title: Donald Trump gives Jake Paul shock endorsement for political career as boxer joins President at Kentucky rally
Boxing.content:
President Donald Trump brought boxing star Jake Paul onstage during a rally on Wednesday and made a bold prediction about the social media star's future.

The 29-year-old Ohio native, who has transitioned from a YouTube personality to a major force in the combat sports world, stood alongside the president as the crowd in Hebron, Ky roared in support.

During the event, Trump signaled that Paul's influence could eventually shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government in the coming years.

'I'm going to make a prediction that you will be, in the not too distant future, running for political office,' Trump told the fighter and the assembled supporters.

The president didn't stop at a mere prediction, immediately offering his political backing to the 'Problem Child' should he decide to pursue a seat in the future.

'And you have my complete and total endorsement,' the president added, cementing a public alliance with one of the most polarizing and popular figures in modern media.

President Donald Trump brought boxing star Jake Paul onstage during a rally on Wednesday

Trump suggested that Paul could shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government

Paul expressed his admiration for the president and credited him with teaching him the fundamental values of grit and resilience

Addressing the Kentucky crowd, Paul expressed his admiration for the president and credited him with teaching him the fundamental values of grit and resilience.

He told the crowd that the president had taught him the value of 'courage' and to 'never back down from a fight,' a mantra Paul has lived by throughout his boxing career.

'We need more factories like this thriving all over the country,' Paul said, emphasizing his support for the president's economic vision for the American Heartland.

'And I know [Trump's] going to be the one to bring that here to us,' he continued, signaling his belief in a resurgence of American manufacturing.

The fighter also leaned into a spiritual message, telling the audience: 'I know God is with us. I know he wants us on the right side of history, and everyone here has to do their part.'

'And God's got us. Trump's got us. God bless,' Paul concluded, before reflecting on the local spirit of the attendees in the Bluegrass State.

'I feel all the local Kentuckians feel the same way -- you guys have that fight, you guys have that swag. There's a lot of young kids in here. The future of America.'

Trump returned the praise, telling the crowd: 'He's a great guy. He's a courageous guy and a talented guy. He's a hell of a fighter, too, by the way.'

'We need more factories like this thriving all over the country,' Paul said, emphasizing his support for the president's economic vision

Paul's most recent endeavor in the boxing ring saw him take on British star Anthony Joshua

The endorsement of Paul's fighting spirit comes as the 29-year-old continues to dominate headlines following his endeavors in the boxing ring over recent years.

Paul has transformed the boxing landscape by leveraging his massive digital following to create events that have outdrawn traditional championship bouts in viewership and revenue.

His most recent endeavor in the ring saw him take on British heavyweight star Anthony Joshua, which ended in a knockout victory for 'AJ'.



Whether Paul follows Trump's advice and swaps the gloves for a suit remains to be seen.

Boxing.title: Anthony Joshua quits the UK to move to Dubai - even as thousands try to flee amid rising panic in Middle East warzone
Boxing.content:
Boxing superstar Anthony Joshua is quitting the UK to move to Dubai - even though the desert city is now in a warzone with thousands of Britons desperate to get out.
